# services.patroni

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.patroni`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.patroni.dataDir` | `absolute path` | Folder where Patroni data will be written, this is where the pgpass password file will be written. |
| `services.patroni.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Patroni. |
| `services.patroni.environmentFiles` | `attribute set of (null or string or absolute path or package)` | Environment variables made available to Patroni as files content, useful for providing secrets from files. |
| `services.patroni.group` | `string` | The group for the service. If left as the default value this group will automatically be created, otherwise the sysadmin is responsible for ensuring the group exists. |
| `services.patroni.name` | `string` | The name of the host. Must be unique for the cluster. |
| `services.patroni.namespace` | `string` | Path within the configuration store where Patroni will keep information about the cluster. |
| `services.patroni.nodeIp` | `string` | IP address of this node. |
| `services.patroni.otherNodesIps` | `list of string` | IP addresses of the other nodes. |
| `services.patroni.postgresqlDataDir` | `absolute path` | The data directory for PostgreSQL. If left as the default value this directory will automatically be created before the PostgreSQL server starts, otherwise the sysadmin is responsible for ensuring the directory exists with appropriate ownership and permissions. |
| `services.patroni.postgresqlPackage` | `package` | PostgreSQL package to use. Plugins can be enabled like this `pkgs.postgresql_14.withPackages (p: [ p.pg_safeupdate p.postgis ])`. |
| `services.patroni.postgresqlPort` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | The port on which PostgreSQL listens. |
| `services.patroni.restApiPort` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | The port on Patroni's REST api listens. |
| `services.patroni.scope` | `string` | Cluster name. |
| `services.patroni.settings` | `YAML 1.1 value` | The primary patroni configuration. See the [documentation](https://patroni.readthedocs.io/en/latest/yaml_configuration.html) for possible values. Secrets should be passed in by using the `environmentFiles` option. |
| `services.patroni.softwareWatchdog` | `boolean` | This will configure Patroni to use the software watchdog built into the Linux kernel as described in the [documentation](https://patroni.readthedocs.io/en/latest/watchdog.html#setting-up-software-watchdog-on-linux). |
| `services.patroni.user` | `string` | The user for the service. If left as the default value this user will automatically be created, otherwise the sysadmin is responsible for ensuring the user exists. |
